 {"id":"bd-1","title":"Phase 1: Project Setup","description":"**EPIC: Project Foundation & Repository Structure**

## Goal
Establish the foundational file structure, versioning, and CI/CD scaffolding that all subsequent work depends on.

## Rationale
A well-organized project structure from day one prevents technical debt accumulation. The giil project demonstrated that a clean, minimal structure with clear separation of concerns makes maintenance and contribution easier. This phase creates the skeleton that gives the project its identity.

## Key Decisions Encoded
- VERSION file as single source of truth (not package.json, not embedded in script)
- MIT License for maximum adoption
- Examples directory for user guidance WITHOUT polluting user's XDG config
- No je_*.txt files in repo (those were development artifacts from original sync script)

## Success Criteria
- Repository has all foundational files in place
- CI can at least do basic syntax validation
- A contributor can clone and understand the structure immediately","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:15:08.191734693-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:15:08.191734693-05:00","close_reason":"Phase 1 setup completed: VERSION, LICENSE, .gitignore exist. Remaining items (bd-104, bd-105, bd-106) can be done in parallel.","labels":["foundation","setup"]}
 {"id":"bd-10","title":"Phase 10: Subcommand Implementations","description":"**EPIC: All Subcommand Implementations**

## Goal
Implement all subcommands: sync, status, init, add, list, doctor, self-update, config.

## Rationale
The subcommand architecture provides clear separation of concerns and discoverable CLI UX. Each command has a single responsibility and can be developed/tested independently.

## Command Overview
- sync: The main event - clone missing repos, pull existing ones
- status: Read-only view of repo states (useful before sync)
- init: First-run setup, creates config dirs and files
- add: Add a repo to the appropriate list file
- list: Show configured repos (with filters)
- doctor: System health check (deps, config, auth)
- self-update: Update ru itself from GitHub releases
- config: View/modify configuration values

## Main Processing Loop (sync/status)
The core loop processes repos sequentially (parallel is v2), tracking results in NDJSON, displaying progress, and handling errors without stopping.

## Success Criteria
- All commands work correctly
- Help text is accurate for each command
- Exit codes are correct per command","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:32:11.731076967-05:00","labels":["commands","core"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-10","depends_on_id":"bd-3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:08.276445145-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-10","depends_on_id":"bd-4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:08.309247652-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-10","depends_on_id":"bd-6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:08.339321557-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-10","depends_on_id":"bd-8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:08.367035466-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-10","depends_on_id":"bd-9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:08.396960711-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1001","title":"Implement cmd_sync()","description":"**Implement sync subcommand - main sync logic**

## What
The main sync command that clones missing repos and pulls existing ones.

## Why
This is the primary function of the tool. Everything else supports this.

## Options
- --clone-only: Only clone new repos, don't pull existing
- --pull-only: Only pull existing repos, don't clone new
- --autostash: Stash local changes before pull
- --rebase: Use rebase instead of ff-only
- --dry-run: Show what would happen

## Flow
1. Ensure dependencies (gh installed and authed)
2. Resolve configuration
3. Load all repos from lists
4. For each repo:
   a. Determine local path
   b. If exists and is git repo: pull
   c. If doesn't exist: clone
   d. If exists but not git repo: warn and skip
5. Print summary

## Acceptance Criteria
- Processes all repos
- Handles errors without stopping
- Reports results correctly
- All options work","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","labels":["commands"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1001","depends_on_id":"bd-10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.542889231-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-1001","depends_on_id":"bd-806","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.572798386-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-1001","depends_on_id":"bd-807","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.602610107-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-1001","depends_on_id":"bd-905","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.632310508-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1002","title":"Implement cmd_status()","description":"**Implement status subcommand - read-only status**

## What
Show the status of all configured repos without making changes.

## Why
Users want to see what sync would do before running it. Also useful for monitoring.

## Options
- --fetch: Fetch first to get accurate ahead/behind (default)
- --no-fetch: Skip fetch for speed

## Output Format
```
Repository              Status      Branch    Ahead/Behind
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
mcp_agent_mail          current     main      0/0
repo_updater            behind      main      0/5
other_project           dirty       main      2/0
```

- Shows all repo statuses
- Accurate ahead/behind with fetch
- Indicates dirty working trees

-{"id":"bd-1003","title":"Implement cmd_add()","description":"**Implement add subcommand - add repo to list**

## What
Add a repository to the appropriate list file.

## Why
Users need an easy way to add repos without editing files manually.

## Options
- --private: Add to private.txt instead of public.txt
- --from-cwd: Add current directory's origin

## Usage
```bash
ru add owner/repo
ru add https://github.com/owner/repo
ru add --private company/internal-repo
ru add --from-cwd  # In a git repo
```

## Implementation
```bash
cmd_add() {
    local repo="$1"
    local private=false
    
    # Parse options
    # ...
    
    # Validate URL/shorthand
    local host owner reponame
    if ! parse_repo_url "$repo" host owner reponame; then
        log_error "Invalid repository: $repo"
        return 4
    fi
    
    # Determine target file
    local target_file
    if [[ "$private" == "true" ]]; then
        target_file="$RU_CONFIG_DIR/repos.d/private.txt"
    else
        target_file="$RU_CONFIG_DIR/repos.d/public.txt"
    fi
    
    # Check for duplicates
    if grep -qF "$owner/$reponame" "$target_file" 2>/dev/null; then
        log_warn "Already in list: $owner/$reponame"
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Append
    echo "$owner/$reponame" >> "$target_file"
    log_success "Added to ${private:+private }list: $owner/$reponame"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Adds to correct list file
- Validates URL format
- Detects duplicates
- --from-cwd extracts from git remote","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","labels":["commands"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1003","depends_on_id":"bd-10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.752541367-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-1003","depends_on_id":"bd-701","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.78216274-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1005","title":"Implement cmd_doctor()","description":"**Implement doctor subcommand - system diagnostics**

## What
Check system configuration and report issues.

## Why
When things don't work, doctor helps diagnose why.

## Checks
1. Git version
2. gh installed and version
3. gh authentication status
4. Config directory exists
5. Repos configured
6. Projects directory exists and writable
7. gum availability (optional)

## Output
```
System Check
────────────────────────────────────────
[OK] git: 2.43.0
[OK] gh: 2.40.1 (authenticated as user)
[OK] Config: ~/.config/ru
[OK] Repos: 47 configured
[OK] Projects: /data/projects (writable)
[  ] gum: not installed (optional)

All checks passed!
```

- Clear pass/fail indicators

 {"id":"bd-1006","title":"Implement cmd_self_update()","description":"**Implement self-update subcommand**

## What
Update ru itself to the latest version from GitHub releases.

## Why
Users shouldn't have to re-run the installer to update.

## Options
- --check: Check for updates without installing

## Flow
1. Get current version from $VERSION
2. Fetch latest release tag from GitHub API
3. Compare versions
4. If newer, download and verify checksum
5. Replace current script

## Implementation Notes
- Use gh api for authenticated requests
- Verify SHA256 checksum
- Atomic replacement (write to temp, then mv)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Checks for updates
- Downloads securely with checksum
- Atomic update (no partial writes)
- --check only reports, doesn't update","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","labels":["commands"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1006","depends_on_id":"bd-10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.965865062-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-1006","depends_on_id":"bd-202","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.995827747-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1007","title":"Implement process_single_repo()","description":"**Orchestrate processing of a single repository**

## What
Function that handles all logic for one repo: check status, decide action, execute.

## Why
Centralizes the per-repo logic that sync and status share.

## Flow
```
1. Parse repo spec (URL, branch, custom name)
2. Resolve local path
3. Check if path exists
4. If exists:
   a. Verify it's a git repo
   b. Check remote URL matches
   c. Get status (behind/ahead/dirty)
   d. Execute action (pull/skip)
5. If not exists:
   a. Clone repo
6. Record result
```

## Implementation
```bash
process_single_repo() {
    local spec="$1"
    local action="$2"  # sync|status
    
    local url branch local_name
    parse_repo_spec "$spec" url branch local_name
    
    local path
    if [[ -n "$local_name" ]]; then
        path="$PROJECTS_DIR/$local_name"
    else
        path=$(url_to_local_path "$url" "$PROJECTS_DIR" "$LAYOUT")
    fi
    
    local repo_name
    repo_name=$(basename "$path")
    
    log_step "Processing: $repo_name"
    
    if [[ -d "$path" ]]; then
        if ! is_git_repo "$path"; then
            log_warn "Not a git repo: $path"
            write_result "$repo_name" "skip" "not_git" "" ""
            return 0
        fi
        
        if ! check_remote_mismatch "$path" "$url"; then
            log_warn "Remote mismatch, skipping: $repo_name"
            write_result "$repo_name" "skip" "remote_mismatch" "" ""
            return 0
        fi
        
        if [[ "$action" == "sync" ]]; then
            do_pull "$path" "$repo_name" "$UPDATE_STRATEGY"
        else
            # status only
            local status
            status=$(get_repo_status "$path" "true")
            # Report status...
        fi
    else
        if [[ "$action" == "sync" ]]; then
            do_clone "$url" "$path" "$repo_name"
        else
            log_info "Not cloned: $repo_name"
            write_result "$repo_name" "status" "missing" "" ""
        fi
    fi
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Handles all cases
- Records all results
- Doesn't stop on errors","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:27:02.632468204-05:00","labels":["commands"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1007","depends_on_id":"bd-801","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:12.025226921-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-1007","depends_on_id":"bd-804","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:12.054912164-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-1007","depends_on_id":"bd-806","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:12.116140631-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-1007","depends_on_id":"bd-807","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:12.150654873-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1008","title":"Implement main processing loop","description":"**Main loop with progress display**

## What
The loop that iterates through all repos with progress indication.

## Why
Users need to see progress during long operations.

## Progress Display
```
→ Processing 12/47: coding_agent_session_search
  ├─ Path: /data/projects/coding_agent_session_search
  ├─ Status: behind (0 ahead, 3 behind)
  ├─ Action: git pull --ff-only
  └─ Result: Updated (2s)
```

## Implementation
```bash
run_processing_loop() {
    local action="$1"  # sync|status
    local repos
    repos=$(get_all_repos)
    
    if [[ -z "$repos" ]]; then
        log_info "No repositories to process."
        return 0
    fi
    
    local total
    total=$(echo "$repos" | wc -l)
    local current=0
    
    while IFS= read -r spec; do
        ((current++))
        log_step "Processing $current/$total: $(basename "$spec")"
        process_single_repo "$spec" "$action"
    done <<< "$repos"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Shows progress (N/M)
- Processes all repos
- Continues on errors
- Works with empty list","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","labels":["commands"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1008","depends_on_id":"bd-1007","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:12.181635576-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-1008","depends_on_id":"bd-905","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:12.212359917-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

## Flow

```
5. If not exists:
   a. Clone repo
6. Record result
```

## Implementation
```bash
process_single_repo() {
    local spec="$1"
    local action="$2"  # sync|status
    
    local url branch local_name
    parse_repo_spec "$spec" url branch local_name
    
    local path
    if [[ -n "$local_name" ]]; then
        path="$PROJECTS_DIR/$local_name"
    else
        path=$(url_to_local_path "$url" "$PROJECTS_DIR" "$LAYOUT")
    fi
    
    local repo_name
    repo_name=$(basename "$path")
    
    log_step "Processing: $repo_name"
    
    if [[ -d "$path" ]]; then
        if ! is_git_repo "$path"; then
            log_warn "Not a git repo: $path"
            write_result "$repo_name" "skip" "not_git" "" ""
            return 0
        fi
        
        if ! check_remote_mismatch "$path" "$url"; then
            log_warn "Remote mismatch, skipping: $repo_name"
            write_result "$repo_name" "skip" "remote_mismatch" "" ""
            return 0
        fi
        
        if [[ "$action" == "sync" ]]; then
            do_pull "$path" "$repo_name" "$UPDATE_STRATEGY"
        else
            # status only
            local status
            status=$(get_repo_status "$path" "true")
            # Report status...
        fi
    else
        if [[ "$action" == "sync" ]]; then
            do_clone "$url" "$path" "$repo_name"
        else
            log_info "Not cloned: $repo_name"
            write_result "$repo_name" "status" "missing" "" ""
        fi
    fi
}
```

- Records all results

+{"id":"bd-1008","title":"Implement main processing loop","description":"**Main loop with progress display**

## What
The loop that iterates through all repos with progress indication.

## Why
Users need to see progress during long operations.

## Progress Display
```
→ Processing 12/47: coding_agent_session_search
  ├─ Path: /data/projects/coding_agent_session_search
  ├─ Status: behind (0 ahead, 3 behind)
  ├─ Action: git pull --ff-only
  └─ Result: Updated (2s)
```

## Implementation
```bash
run_processing_loop() {
    local action="$1"  # sync|status
    local repos
    repos=$(get_all_repos)
    
    if [[ -z "$repos" ]]; then
        log_info "No repositories to process."
        return 0
    fi
    
    local total
    total=$(echo "$repos" | wc -l)
    local current=0
    
    while IFS= read -r spec; do
        ((current++))
        log_step "Processing $current/$total: $(basename "$spec")"
        process_single_repo "$spec" "$action"
    done <<< "$repos"
}
```

 {"id":"bd-1009","title":"Implement cmd_remove()","description":"**Implement remove subcommand - remove repo from list**

## What
The `ru remove` command to remove a repository from the list.

## Why
Users need to remove repos without manually editing list files. This is a fundamental CRUD operation that was missing from the original plan.

## Usage
```bash
ru remove owner/repo
ru remove owner/repo --all  # Remove from all lists
ru remove --from-cwd        # Remove current directory's repo
```

## Implementation
```bash
cmd_remove() {
    local repo="$1"
    local list_file="$RU_CONFIG_DIR/repos.d/repos.txt"
    
    if [[ ! -f "$list_file" ]]; then
        log_error "No repos configured"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Find and remove matching line
    local pattern
    pattern=$(echo "$repo" | sed 's/[.[\/^$*]/\\&/g')
    
    if grep -q "$pattern" "$list_file"; then
        sed -i "/$pattern/d" "$list_file"
        log_success "Removed: $repo"
    else
        log_warn "Not found in list: $repo"
        return 1
    fi
}
```

- Handles 'not found' gracefully
- Works with partial matches (owner/repo matches full URL)

 {"id":"bd-1010","title":"Implement cmd_prune()","description":"**Implement prune subcommand - find orphan repos**

## What
The `ru prune` command to identify and optionally archive repos in PROJECTS_DIR that aren't in any list.

## Why
Over time, users clone repos manually or remove entries from lists. These 'orphan' repos waste disk space and cause confusion. The prune command helps identify them.

## Usage
```bash
ru prune              # List orphans (dry run)
ru prune --archive    # Move orphans to ~/.local/state/ru/archived/
ru prune --delete     # Actually delete (requires confirmation)
```

## Implementation
```bash
cmd_prune() {
    local action="${1:-list}"
    local configured_paths
    configured_paths=$(get_all_repos | while read spec; do
        url_to_local_path "$spec" "$PROJECTS_DIR" "$LAYOUT"
    done | sort -u)
    
    # Find all git repos in PROJECTS_DIR
    local actual_repos
    actual_repos=$(find "$PROJECTS_DIR" -maxdepth 2 -name .git -type d | \
        xargs -I{} dirname {} | sort -u)
    
    # Find orphans (in actual but not in configured)
    local orphans
    orphans=$(comm -23 <(echo "$actual_repos") <(echo "$configured_paths"))
    
    if [[ -z "$orphans" ]]; then
        log_info "No orphan repositories found"
        return 0
    fi
    
    echo "$orphans" | while read path; do
        log_warn "Orphan: $path"
    done
    
    # Handle archive/delete based on action
}
```

## Safety
- Default is LIST ONLY (dry run)
- Archive moves to dated subfolder, never deletes
- Delete requires explicit --delete AND confirmation

## Acceptance Criteria
- Identifies repos not in any list
- Safe default (list only)
- Archive preserves repos safely
- Never deletes without explicit confirmation","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","labels":["commands"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1010","depends_on_id":"bd-10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.465248731-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-1010","depends_on_id":"bd-905","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.496361533-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-1010","depends_on_id":"bd-703","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.527218564-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-809","title":"Handle branch not found gracefully","description":"**Graceful handling when repo@branch branch doesn't exist**

## What
When user specifies repo@branch and branch doesn't exist, handle gracefully.

## Why
Branches get deleted, renamed, or typos happen. Current plan doesn't address this.

## Behavior
```
$ ru sync owner/repo@nonexistent
Cloning: owner/repo
  Warning: Branch 'nonexistent' not found
  Falling back to default branch (main)
```

## Implementation
```bash
do_clone() {
    # ... clone repo ...
    
    if [[ -n "$branch" ]]; then
        if git -C "$target_dir" rev-parse --verify "origin/$branch" &>/dev/null; then
            git -C "$target_dir" checkout "$branch" --quiet
        else
            log_warn "Branch '$branch' not found, using default branch"
            write_result "$repo_name" "clone" "branch_not_found" "" "Branch: $branch"
        fi
    fi
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Clone succeeds with warning
- Default branch used as fallback
- Result recorded for summary
- User informed clearly","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","labels":["error-handling","git"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-809","depends_on_id":"bd-8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.797824099-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-809","depends_on_id":"bd-902","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.828864235-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

## What
Add timeout handling for clone and pull operations.

## Why
Flaky networks, slow connections, or overloaded servers can cause operations to hang indefinitely. Users need a way to bound wait time.

## Implementation
- Set GIT_HTTP_LOW_SPEED_LIMIT=1000 and GIT_HTTP_LOW_SPEED_TIME=30 as defaults
- These cause git to abort if transfer rate drops below 1KB/s for 30 seconds
- Add --timeout N option to override (sets GIT_HTTP_LOW_SPEED_TIME)
- On timeout, log error and continue to next repo
- Report timeouts distinctly in summary (not just 'failed')

-{"id":"bd-9","title":"Phase 9: Repo List Management","description":"**EPIC: Repository List Loading & Management**

## Goal
Implement robust loading, parsing, and deduplication of repository lists from config files.

## Rationale
Users maintain lists of repos to sync. These lists support comments, blank lines, branch pinning (repo@branch), and custom local names (repo as custom-name). We must parse all this robustly and handle edge cases.

## List File Format
```
# Comments start with #
# Blank lines are ignored

https://github.com/owner/repo
owner/repo
owner/repo@develop
owner/repo as custom-name
```

## Deduplication
Multiple list files may reference the same repo. We dedupe by resolved local path, keeping the first occurrence. This prevents double-processing.

## Future: Tags
We're reserving syntax for tags: `owner/repo #tag=backend`. Not implementing now, but the parser should not break on this.

## Success Criteria
- List files parse correctly with all syntax variants
- Comments and blank lines are skipped
- Duplicates are detected and deduplicated
- Collisions are warned about","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:30:21.319145702-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:30:21.319145702-05:00","close_reason":"All repo list management functions implemented: load_repo_list, parse_repo_spec, dedupe_repos, detect_collisions, get_all_repos","labels":["lists","repos"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9","depends_on_id":"bd-3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:08.218431105-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-9","depends_on_id":"bd-7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:08.246730918-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"bd-901","title":"Implement load_repo_list()","description":"**Parse repository list file**

## What
Function to load repos from a list file, skipping comments and blank lines.

## Why
List files are the primary way users specify repos. We need robust parsing.

## List Format
```
# Comment lines
# Blank lines ignored

https://github.com/owner/repo
owner/repo
owner/repo@branch
owner/repo as custom-name
```

## Implementation
```bash
load_repo_list() {
    local file="$1"
    
    if [[ ! -f "$file" ]]; then
        return 0  # Empty list, not an error
    fi
    
    while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
        # Skip empty lines and comments
        [[ -z "$line" || "$line" =~ ^[[:space:]]*# ]] && continue
        
        # Trim whitespace
        line="${line#"${line%%[![:space:]]*}"}"
        line="${line%"${line##*[![:space:]]}"}"
        
        echo "$line"
    done < "$file"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Skips comments (#)
- Skips blank lines
- Trims whitespace
- Handles missing file gracefully","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:30:11.240443554-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:30:11.240443554-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented in ru script SECTION 9B","labels":["lists"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-901","depends_on_id":"bd-9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.244259733-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"bd-902","title":"Implement parse_repo_spec()","description":"**Parse repo specification with optional branch/name**

## What
Function to parse `repo@branch` and `repo as custom-name` syntax.

## Why
Users need to pin branches and customize local directory names.

## Syntax Patterns
- `owner/repo` -> repo, default branch, repo as name
- `owner/repo@develop` -> repo, develop branch, repo as name
- `owner/repo as myname` -> repo, default branch, myname as name
- `owner/repo@develop as myname` -> repo, develop branch, myname as name

## Implementation
```bash
parse_repo_spec() {
    local spec="$1"
    local -n _url=$2
    local -n _branch=$3
    local -n _local_name=$4
    
    # Extract 'as <name>' if present
    if [[ "$spec" =~ ^(.+)[[:space:]]+as[[:space:]]+(.+)$ ]]; then
        spec="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
        _local_name="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
    else
        _local_name=""
    fi
    
    # Extract '@branch' if present
    if [[ "$spec" =~ ^(.+)@([^@]+)$ ]]; then
        _url="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
        _branch="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
    else
        _url="$spec"
        _branch=""
    fi
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All syntax patterns work
- Returns empty for optional parts
- Handles edge cases","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:30:11.245870708-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:30:11.245870708-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented in ru script SECTION 9B","labels":["lists","parsing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-902","depends_on_id":"bd-701","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.274168778-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-902","depends_on_id":"bd-901","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.302284824-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"bd-903","title":"Implement dedupe_repos()","description":"**Remove duplicate repositories by local path**

## What
Function to deduplicate repos that would resolve to the same local path.

## Why
Multiple list files or formats might reference the same repo. We process each path only once.

## Implementation
```bash
dedupe_repos() {
    # Input: lines of repo specs
    # Output: unique repo specs by resolved path
    
    local -A seen_paths
    
    while IFS= read -r spec; do
        local url branch local_name
        parse_repo_spec "$spec" url branch local_name
        
        local path
        if [[ -n "$local_name" ]]; then
            path="$PROJECTS_DIR/$local_name"
        else
            path=$(url_to_local_path "$url" "$PROJECTS_DIR" "$LAYOUT")
        fi
        
        if [[ -z "${seen_paths[$path]:-}" ]]; then
            seen_paths[$path]=1
            echo "$spec"
        else
            log_debug "Skipping duplicate: $spec (same path as previous)"
        fi
    done
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- First occurrence kept
- Duplicates logged (debug)
- Works with custom names","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:30:11.247069987-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:30:11.247069987-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented in ru script SECTION 9B","labels":["lists"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-903","depends_on_id":"bd-703","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.334028255-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-903","depends_on_id":"bd-901","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.363694662-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"bd-904","title":"Implement detect_collisions()","description":"**Warn about path collisions**

## What
Function to detect when different repos would resolve to the same local path.

## Why
With flat layout, org1/utils and org2/utils collide. Users need to know before we overwrite.

## Difference from dedupe
- dedupe: Same repo referenced twice (ok, skip second)
- collision: Different repos mapping to same path (warning!)

## Implementation
```bash
detect_collisions() {
    local -A path_to_url
    local collisions=0
    
    while IFS= read -r spec; do
        local url branch local_name
        parse_repo_spec "$spec" url branch local_name
        
        local path
        path=$(url_to_local_path "$url" "$PROJECTS_DIR" "$LAYOUT")
        
        local norm_url
        norm_url=$(normalize_url "$url")
        
        if [[ -n "${path_to_url[$path]:-}" ]]; then
            local existing="${path_to_url[$path]}"
            if [[ "$existing" != "$norm_url" ]]; then
                log_warn "Collision detected: $path"
                log_warn "  URL 1: $existing"
                log_warn "  URL 2: $norm_url"
                ((collisions++))
            fi
        else
            path_to_url[$path]="$norm_url"
        fi
    done
    
    return $collisions
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Detects different repos at same path
- Doesn't flag same repo twice
- Returns collision count","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:30:11.248169378-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:30:11.248169378-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented in ru script SECTION 9B","labels":["lists","safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-904","depends_on_id":"bd-703","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.391710099-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-904","depends_on_id":"bd-901","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.422048533-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"bd-905","title":"Implement get_all_repos()","description":"**Load and merge all repository lists**

## What
Function to load all list files, merge, dedupe, and check for collisions.

## Why
This is the main entry point for getting the list of repos to process.

## Implementation
```bash
get_all_repos() {
    local repos_dir="$RU_CONFIG_DIR/repos.d"
    
    if [[ ! -d "$repos_dir" ]]; then
        log_warn "No repos.d directory. Run 'ru init' first."
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Collect all repos from all .txt files
    local all_repos
    all_repos=$(
        for f in "$repos_dir"/*.txt; do
            [[ -f "$f" ]] || continue
            load_repo_list "$f"
        done
    )
    
    if [[ -z "$all_repos" ]]; then
        log_warn "No repositories configured. Run 'ru add <repo>' first."
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Check for collisions (warning only)
    echo "$all_repos" | detect_collisions || true
    
    # Dedupe and output
    echo "$all_repos" | dedupe_repos
}
```

+{"id":"bd-9","title":"Phase 9: Repo List Management","description":"**EPIC: Repository List Loading & Management**

## Goal
Implement robust loading, parsing, and deduplication of repository lists from config files.

## Rationale
Users maintain lists of repos to sync. These lists support comments, blank lines, branch pinning (repo@branch), and custom local names (repo as custom-name). We must parse all this robustly and handle edge cases.

## List File Format
```
# Comments start with #
# Blank lines are ignored

https://github.com/owner/repo
owner/repo
owner/repo@develop
owner/repo as custom-name
```

## Deduplication
Multiple list files may reference the same repo. We dedupe by resolved local path, keeping the first occurrence. This prevents double-processing.

## Future: Tags
We're reserving syntax for tags: `owner/repo #tag=backend`. Not implementing now, but the parser should not break on this.

## Success Criteria
- List files parse correctly with all syntax variants
- Comments and blank lines are skipped
- Duplicates are detected and deduplicated
- Collisions are warned about","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:33:03.268499861-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:33:03.268499861-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all repo list management functions: parse_repo_spec (namerefs), load_repo_list, dedupe_repos, detect_collisions, get_all_repos. +{"id":"bd-901","title":"Implement load_repo_list()","description":"**Parse repository list file**

## What
Function to load repos from a list file, skipping comments and blank lines.

## Why
List files are the primary way users specify repos. We need robust parsing.

## List Format
```
# Comment lines
# Blank lines ignored

https://github.com/owner/repo
owner/repo
owner/repo@branch
owner/repo as custom-name
```

## Implementation
```bash
load_repo_list() {
    local file="$1"
    
    if [[ ! -f "$file" ]]; then
        return 0  # Empty list, not an error
    fi
    
    while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
        # Skip empty lines and comments
        [[ -z "$line" || "$line" =~ ^[[:space:]]*# ]] && continue
        
        # Trim whitespace
        line="${line#"${line%%[![:space:]]*}"}"
        line="${line%"${line##*[![:space:]]}"}"
        
        echo "$line"
    done < "$file"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Skips comments (#)
- Skips blank lines
- Trims whitespace
- Handles missing file gracefully","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:33:03.238664072-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:33:03.238664072-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all repo list management functions: parse_repo_spec (namerefs), load_repo_list, dedupe_repos, detect_collisions, get_all_repos. All tests pass.","labels":["lists"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-901","depends_on_id":"bd-9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.244259733-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"bd-902","title":"Implement parse_repo_spec()","description":"**Parse repo specification with optional branch/name**

## What
Function to parse `repo@branch` and `repo as custom-name` syntax.

## Why
Users need to pin branches and customize local directory names.

## Syntax Patterns
- `owner/repo` -> repo, default branch, repo as name
- `owner/repo@develop` -> repo, develop branch, repo as name
- `owner/repo as myname` -> repo, default branch, myname as name
- `owner/repo@develop as myname` -> repo, develop branch, myname as name

## Implementation
```bash
parse_repo_spec() {
    local spec="$1"
    local -n _url=$2
    local -n _branch=$3
    local -n _local_name=$4
    
    # Extract 'as <name>' if present
    if [[ "$spec" =~ ^(.+)[[:space:]]+as[[:space:]]+(.+)$ ]]; then
        spec="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
        _local_name="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
    else
        _local_name=""
    fi
    
    # Extract '@branch' if present
    if [[ "$spec" =~ ^(.+)@([^@]+)$ ]]; then
        _url="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
        _branch="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
    else
        _url="$spec"
        _branch=""
    fi
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All syntax patterns work
- Returns empty for optional parts
- Handles edge cases","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:33:03.262321733-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:33:03.262321733-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all repo list management functions: parse_repo_spec (namerefs), load_repo_list, dedupe_repos, detect_collisions, get_all_repos. +{"id":"bd-903","title":"Implement dedupe_repos()","description":"**Remove duplicate repositories by local path**

## What
Function to deduplicate repos that would resolve to the same local path.

## Why
Multiple list files or formats might reference the same repo. We process each path only once.

## Implementation
```bash
dedupe_repos() {
    # Input: lines of repo specs
    # Output: unique repo specs by resolved path
    
    local -A seen_paths
    
    while IFS= read -r spec; do
        local url branch local_name
        parse_repo_spec "$spec" url branch local_name
        
        local path
        if [[ -n "$local_name" ]]; then
            path="$PROJECTS_DIR/$local_name"
        else
            path=$(url_to_local_path "$url" "$PROJECTS_DIR" "$LAYOUT")
        fi
        
        if [[ -z "${seen_paths[$path]:-}" ]]; then
            seen_paths[$path]=1
            echo "$spec"
        else
            log_debug "Skipping duplicate: $spec (same path as previous)"
        fi
    done
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- First occurrence kept
- Duplicates logged (debug)
- Works with custom names","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:33:03.26408771-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:33:03.26408771-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all repo list management functions: parse_repo_spec (namerefs), load_repo_list, dedupe_repos, detect_collisions, get_all_repos. All tests pass.","labels":["lists"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-903","depends_on_id":"bd-703","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.334028255-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-903","depends_on_id":"bd-901","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.363694662-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"bd-904","title":"Implement detect_collisions()","description":"**Warn about path collisions**

## What
Function to detect when different repos would resolve to the same local path.

## Why
With flat layout, org1/utils and org2/utils collide. Users need to know before we overwrite.

## Difference from dedupe
- dedupe: Same repo referenced twice (ok, skip second)
- collision: Different repos mapping to same path (warning!)

## Implementation
```bash
detect_collisions() {
    local -A path_to_url
    local collisions=0
    
    while IFS= read -r spec; do
        local url branch local_name
        parse_repo_spec "$spec" url branch local_name
        
        local path
        path=$(url_to_local_path "$url" "$PROJECTS_DIR" "$LAYOUT")
        
        local norm_url
        norm_url=$(normalize_url "$url")
        
        if [[ -n "${path_to_url[$path]:-}" ]]; then
            local existing="${path_to_url[$path]}"
            if [[ "$existing" != "$norm_url" ]]; then
                log_warn "Collision detected: $path"
                log_warn "  URL 1: $existing"
                log_warn "  URL 2: $norm_url"
                ((collisions++))
            fi
        else
            path_to_url[$path]="$norm_url"
        fi
    done
    
    return $collisions
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Detects different repos at same path
- Doesn't flag same repo twice
- Returns collision count","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:33:03.266022614-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:33:03.266022614-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all repo list management functions: parse_repo_spec (namerefs), load_repo_list, dedupe_repos, detect_collisions, get_all_repos. All tests pass.","labels":["lists","safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-904","depends_on_id":"bd-703","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.391710099-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-904","depends_on_id":"bd-901","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.422048533-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"bd-905","title":"Implement get_all_repos()","description":"**Load and merge all repository lists**

## What
Function to load all list files, merge, dedupe, and check for collisions.

## Why
This is the main entry point for getting the list of repos to process.

## Implementation
```bash
get_all_repos() {
    local repos_dir="$RU_CONFIG_DIR/repos.d"
    
    if [[ ! -d "$repos_dir" ]]; then
        log_warn "No repos.d directory. Run 'ru init' first."
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Collect all repos from all .txt files
    local all_repos
    all_repos=$(
        for f in "$repos_dir"/*.txt; do
            [[ -f "$f" ]] || continue
            load_repo_list "$f"
        done
    )
    
    if [[ -z "$all_repos" ]]; then
        log_warn "No repositories configured. Run 'ru add <repo>' first."
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Check for collisions (warning only)
    echo "$all_repos" | detect_collisions || true
    
    # Dedupe and output
    echo "$all_repos" | dedupe_repos
}
```

- Loads from all .txt files in repos.d
- Warns about collisions

 {"id":"bd-905b","title":"Define collision handling policy","description":"**Clear policy for path collision handling**

## What
Define what happens when collision is detected (different repos -> same path).

## Why
Current plan detects collisions but doesn't specify the behavior.

## Policy Options
1. **Warn and skip second repo** (safest, current implicit behavior)
2. **Fail fast** (strict, stops processing)
3. **Prompt user** (interactive only)
4. **Auto-rename with suffix** (risky)

## Chosen Policy: Warn and skip
```
Collision detected:
  Path: /home/user/projects/utils
  Configured: org1/utils (will be synced)
  Skipped:    org2/utils (same path)

To fix: Change layout to 'owner-repo' in config:
  ru config --set LAYOUT=owner-repo
```

## Implementation
- First occurrence wins
- Log warning with resolution hint
- Include in summary as 'skipped:collision'
- Exit code 2 (conflicts) if any collisions

## Acceptance Criteria
- Clear messaging about collision
- First-wins policy
- Resolution hint in output
- Documented in README","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:30:11.250738889-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:30:11.250738889-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented in ru script SECTION 9B","labels":["lists","safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-905b","depends_on_id":"bd-9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.858988726-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-905b","depends_on_id":"bd-904","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.890572977-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
